The classification of Eleogiton scabra reflects its unique adaptations to aquatic environments and its relationships with other plant species.

intro: Eleogiton scabra, also known as rough water-plantain or rough water-plantain, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the family Alismataceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities, which can help in treating various skin conditions and infections. The bioactive constituents of Eleogiton scabra include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
